title: Storm Drain Adaptive Penstock Gate Controller
description: Monitors storm drain water depth with a weatherproof ultrasonic sensor, computes real-time channel flow rate via Manning's equation, and controls a linear actuator penstock gate to maintain flow below 85% of channel capacity — including first-flush diversion logic and flood bypass mode, distinct from rainwater harvesting quality-monitoring projects.
objective: Adaptive gate control to keep channel flow below 85% of Manning capacity, divert first 10 mm first-flush runoff, and trigger flood bypass at 95%, with SD event log.
constraints: Arduino Uno, JSN-SR04T sensor, L298N H-bridge, 200 mm linear actuator, 300 mm penstock, SD card; 0.3 m wide concrete channel (n=0.013), slope S=0.005; 12 V solar battery.
supporting_fact_titles: Manning's Equation for Open Channel Flow Capacity
design_element_titles: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller

element_usage_notes.JSN-SR04T Weatherproof Ultrasonic Depth: Measures water depth; combined with known rectangular geometry yields flow area and hydraulic radius.
element_usage_notes.Linear Actuator Penstock Gate Drive: Three-position gate control driven by H-bridge based on flow ratio and first-flush timer.
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Outer loop: flow ratio vs. 0.80 capacity setpoint; inner loop: gate position rate of change.
element_usage_notes.Buzzer and LED Fault Annunciator: Flood alarm at 95% capacity or sensor out-of-range fault.

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Flow rate computed from depth fed as outer PID loop process variable.

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: PID output maps to gate position command (0=closed, 1=partial, 2=full).
Flow ratio exceeding 0.95 independently triggers flood alarm.
